description: Working on the customer authentication and account lifecycle for both consumer and partner channels, plus the wider reservation platform around it.
items: Built the customer authentication and account lifecycle on Kotlin/Spring Boot with Keycloak / Red Hat SSO - account creation, email verification, password reset, login, logout, account recovery, profile updates, and account deletion.
Set up Keycloak as the shared identity layer for B2B and B2C, integrating customer profiles into a single source for downstream personalisation and segmentation.
Worked across the wider reservation platform alongside the auth work.
Rewrote slow PostgreSQL queries on the auth and account services and added composite indexes where lookups were doing full scans.
results: Authored a C4-model architecture POC using Structurizr DSL and presented it to the team as a proposal for living architecture documentation; adopted by individual developers interested in learning the DSL.
Modernised the team's Gradle build setup with multi-level CI caching, cutting build times and simplifying the architecture pattern - adopted as the internal reference by 3+ adjacent teams.
selectedWorkBlurb: Customer authentication and account lifecycle on Kotlin + Spring Boot with Keycloak — for both consumer and B2B partner organisations.

description: Short engagement building a data quality tool that scanned Dassault's global online sales and product-referential systems for missing fields, broken references, and inconsistent values across millions of records.
items: Built a data quality tool that scanned Dassault’s global online sales and product-referential systems for missing fields, broken references, and inconsistent values across millions of records.

Started this tool as a Python/SQL prototype, then rewrote it as a Java/Spring Boot service integrated into the release pipeline, partnering with business analysts to translate vague data-quality rules into automated checks.

company.name: Audi AG, Ingolstadt — via Lunatech SAS

description: Built Audi's in-house ground-truth labelling platform and data pipeline for autonomous-driving model training. Worked remotely from Paris on a fast-growing team.
items: Built Audi's in-house ground-truth labelling platform and data pipeline for autonomous-driving model training — handling vehicle video ingestion, frame extraction, labelling job distribution, and export of finished labels to training systems.
Orchestrated batch and asynchronous workflows with Argo Workflow, integrated with the cloud platform so model predictions could pre-label data and speed up annotation.
Streamed labeling and annotation events through Kafka, indexed them into Elasticsearch to power dataset search, slicing, and quality dashboards used by ML and QA teams.
Set the backend architecture early on so the codebase could absorb fast team growth without re-litigating structure later.
results: That early architectural call paid off as the team grew — multiple developers came onto the project later and could ship features quickly without spending time fighting the codebase or relitigating structure.
selectedWorkBlurb: Set the early backend architecture for Audi's in-house labeling platform — ground-truth video data feeding autonomous-driving model training.

description: Helped lead the technical pilot for D4th, the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the Disneyland Paris public site (~10 million annual visitors).
items: Helped lead the technical pilot (internally known as D4th — Disney 4th gen), the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the Disneyland Paris public site (~10 million annual visitors).
The pilot landed well enough that Disney funded a multi-million-euro programme on the back of it to fully replace the legacy marketing application.
Wrote supporting microservices for browser push notifications and the SOAP integration with the older internal systems.
results: Took on GitLab CI/CD setup and production deployment myself, even though both were outside my original scope — figured out the existing infrastructure, ran the initial deployments, and handed it off cleanly when a more experienced infra engineer joined later.
selectedWorkBlurb: Helped lead the technical pilot for D4th — the new marketing platform integrating Tridion CMS with the public site (~10M annual visitors). Pilot funded a multi-million-euro replacement of the legacy app.

description: Worked remotely on a B2B shipment-lifecycle platform — new features, maintenance, and day-to-day operations.
items: Added a Quartz-based scheduler that pushed automated reports to operations so delivery issues could be caught earlier.
Deployed to AWS EC2 and handled day-to-day production: log analysis and incident triage.

company.name: Nokia Bell Labs (formerly Alcatel-Lucent) — Paris, France

description: Internship in the research division working on video-quality estimation and streaming protocols.
items: Built a video-quality estimation tool in Java implementing the ITU-T P.1201.1 model.
Analysed streaming traffic with Wireshark and tcpdump as part of research into QUIC, HTTP/2, and H.264.
